name: GT-opnorm-submult
source: Kitaev (arXiv:2405.02434), refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:638-642 (Sec. 'Basic properties of eps-Banach algebras'), VERBATIM: "\|w\|=\sup_{\|Z\|\le 1}\|w(Z)\|,\qquad w\colon \calA\to\calA," ... "and bounded linear operators form a Banach algebra." This fixes (i) the OPERATOR NORM on linear operators w:A->A as ||w||=sup_{||Z||<=1}||w(Z)||, and (ii) that bounded linear operators form a BANACH ALGEBRA. SUBMULTIPLICATIVITY ||uw||<=||u||*||w|| is then immediate from the operator-norm definition: for ||Z||<=1, ||(uw)(Z)||=||u(w(Z))||<=||u||*||w(Z)||<=||u||*||w||*||Z||<=||u||*||w|| (using ||w(Z)||<=||w||*||Z|| applied twice), so taking sup over ||Z||<=1 gives ||uw||<=||u||*||w||; equivalently this is the defining Banach-algebra norm inequality. Used at the eps=0 (exact-associativity, ordinary B(H)) specialization relevant here.
